title: Deep Golden Spanish Onion Skillet
description: A traditional Galician dish featuring caramelized yellow onions, this recipe is a umami-rich and satisfying solo dinner option. The use of a mortar and pestle to pound the onions adds a unique texture and depth of flavor.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat.
Add the sliced onions to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes or until they are deep golden brown and caramelized.
Use a mortar and pestle to pound the cooked onions into a rough paste.
Add the minced garlic, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the mortar and pestle and pound until well combined.
Add the onion paste back to the skillet and stir to combine with any remaining oil and spices.
Pour in the chicken broth and stir to deglaze the skillet, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the skillet hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
